types.name: Character
types.description: A sentient being who acts, speaks, or is acted upon.

types.name: Location
types.description: A place where events occur.

types.name: Object
types.description: A significant item in the world.

types.name: Event
types.description: A discrete occurrence in the timeline.

types.name: Language
types.description: A linguistic system with phonology, morphology, syntax, and writing system. Properties follow a defined structure.

types.name: Lexicon Entry
types.description: A word or phrase from a language. Modeled as a separate node because lexicon entries are referenced from passages, annotated, and culturally contextualized.

types.name: Language Register
types.description: A speech register or dialect of a language. Modeled as a separate node because voice configurations reference registers.

types.name: Speaks
types.description: Language capability — source character speaks target language.

types.name: Has Register
types.description: Links a language to one of its registers or dialects.

types.name: Has Term
types.description: Links a language to a lexicon entry.

types.name: Kinship
types.description: A familial relationship between characters.

types.name: Relationship
types.description: A non-familial social bond between characters.

types.name: Conflict
types.description: An adversarial relationship between characters.

types.name: Participation
types.description: A character's involvement in an event.

types.name: Spatial
types.description: A spatial relationship between world elements.

types.name: Ownership
types.description: Possession or control of an entity by another.

types.name: Simple
types.description: A generic directed relationship.

valueDefs.name: Colloquial
valueDefs.description: Informal, everyday language.

valueDefs.name: Formal
valueDefs.description: Formal, elevated language register.

valueDefs.name: Warrior Terse
valueDefs.description: Short imperative constructions typical of Klingon battle speech. OVS with minimal suffixes.

valueDefs.name: Clipped Speech
valueDefs.description: Abbreviated speech with dropped prefixes and reduced suffixes. Typical of informal Klingon.

valueDefs.name: Ceremonial
valueDefs.description: Ritualistic, archaic language used in ceremonies and oaths.

valueDefs.name: Klingon Romanized
valueDefs.description: Klingon language rendered in standard Okrand romanization.

valueDefs.name: Act
valueDefs.description: A major division of a dramatic work.

valueDefs.name: Hook
valueDefs.description: An opening that draws the audience in.

valueDefs.name: Inciting Incident
valueDefs.description: The event that sets the main conflict in motion.

valueDefs.name: Rising Action
valueDefs.description: Escalating tension toward the climax.

valueDefs.name: Climax
valueDefs.description: The peak moment of tension or conflict.

valueDefs.name: Resolution
valueDefs.description: The story's conclusion and aftermath.

valueDefs.name: Continuation
valueDefs.description: A smooth transition continuing the flow.

valueDefs.name: Cut
valueDefs.description: An abrupt transition between scenes.

valueDefs.name: Canon
valueDefs.description: The canonical version of events.

valueDefs.name: Film
valueDefs.description: A feature-length motion picture format.

valueDefs.name: Screenplay
valueDefs.description: A written work for film or television.

valueDefs.name: Theme
valueDefs.description: A recurring thematic thread.

valueDefs.name: Chekhov's Gun
valueDefs.description: An element introduced early that becomes significant later.

valueDefs.name: Setup-Payoff
valueDefs.description: A narrative device where an early setup leads to a later payoff.

valueDefs.name: Dramatic Irony
valueDefs.description: The audience knows something the characters do not.

valueDefs.name: Foreign Text
valueDefs.description: A text span in a language other than the passage's primary language.

valueDefs.name: Names
valueDefs.description: A text span that names or identifies a world element.

valueDefs.name: Describes
valueDefs.description: A text span that describes properties of a world element.

valueDefs.name: Implies
valueDefs.description: A text span that implies or references a world relationship.

nodes.name: tlhIngan Hol

nodeType.name: Language
nodeType.description: A linguistic system with phonology, morphology, syntax, and writing system. Properties follow a defined structure.

nodes.description: The warrior language of the Klingon Empire, created by linguist Marc Okrand for Star Trek III. Agglutinative, OVS word order, no adjectives. The first constructed language designed for a major motion picture.

syntax.adjective_equivalent: Stative verbs follow the noun they modify
syntax.comparison: A Q law' B Q puS
syntax.questions: Suffix -'a' on verb, or question words (nuq, 'Iv, ghorgh, nuqDaq)
syntax.relative_clauses: -bogh on verb, head noun marked with -'e'

writing_system.name: pIqaD
writing_system.case_sensitivity: Romanization is case-sensitive: D/S are retroflex, H is velar fricative, I is near-close front, Q is uvular affricate

pragmatics.greetings: nuqneH (What do you want?) — the only greeting
pragmatics.politeness: Rudeness is default. Politeness marked by rare -neS suffix.

hasTag.name: Klingon
hasTag.description: A member of the Klingon species or related to Klingon culture.

hasTag.description: An object or concept with deep cultural significance.

toward.name: David Marcus

nodeType.name: Character
nodeType.description: A sentient being who acts, speaks, or is acted upon.

toward.description: Kirk's son and co-creator of the Genesis Device. His use of unstable protomatter in the Genesis matrix is the planet's fatal flaw. Killed by Klingons.

hasTag.name: Starfleet
hasTag.description: Associated with the United Federation of Planets Starfleet.

hasTag.description: Involves personal sacrifice or loss for a greater purpose.

anchor.name: Kruge

nodeType.name: Character
nodeType.description: A sentient being who acts, speaks, or is acted upon.

anchor.description: Klingon commander obsessed with obtaining the Genesis secret as a weapon. Ruthless, cunning, and speaks exclusively in Klingon with his crew. Commands a Bird of Prey.

hasTag.name: Antagonist
hasTag.description: A character opposing the protagonist.

hasTag.name: Klingon
hasTag.description: A member of the Klingon species or related to Klingon culture.

toward.name: Genesis Device

nodeType.name: Object
nodeType.description: A significant item in the world.

toward.description: A terraforming technology that reorganizes matter at the subatomic level. Created by David Marcus and Carol Marcus. Its flawed protomatter matrix causes the Genesis Planet's instability.

hasTag.name: Sci-Fi
hasTag.description: Science fiction element or technology.

vocabularyLevel.name: Warrior Terse
vocabularyLevel.description: Short imperative constructions typical of Klingon battle speech. OVS with minimal suffixes.

sentenceTendency.name: Clipped Speech
sentenceTendency.description: Abbreviated speech with dropped prefixes and reduced suffixes. Typical of informal Klingon.

renderingMode.name: Klingon Romanized
renderingMode.description: Klingon language rendered in standard Okrand romanization.

devices.description: David's use of protomatter is revealed as the cause of Genesis Planet's rapid aging. The planet's disintegration creates the ticking clock for the climax.

nodeType.description: A discrete occurrence in the timeline.

nodeIds.description: The Enterprise arrives at the Genesis Planet to find Spock's regenerated body and the planet rapidly aging and becoming unstable.

passages.content:
INT. KLINGON BIRD OF PREY - BRIDGE

KRUGE stands at the command chair. The Genesis Planet fills the viewscreen.

MALTZ
(in Klingon, subtitled)
joH, Daq Samlu'.

KRUGE
(in Klingon, subtitled)
QaQ. Genesis vIghaj vIneH. Qapla'!

valueDefs.name: Colloquial
valueDefs.description: Informal, everyday language.

valueDefs.name: Formal
valueDefs.description: Formal, elevated language register.

valueDefs.name: Warrior Terse
valueDefs.description: Short imperative constructions typical of Klingon battle speech. OVS with minimal suffixes.

valueDefs.name: Clipped Speech
valueDefs.description: Abbreviated speech with dropped prefixes and reduced suffixes. Typical of informal Klingon.

valueDefs.name: Ceremonial
valueDefs.description: Ritualistic, archaic language used in ceremonies and oaths.

valueDefs.name: Klingon Romanized
valueDefs.description: Klingon language rendered in standard Okrand romanization.

valueDefs.description: The peak moment of tension or conflict.

nodes.name: tlhIngan Hol

nodeType.name: Language
nodeType.description: A linguistic system with phonology, morphology, syntax, and writing system. Properties follow a defined structure.

nodes.description: The warrior language of the Klingon Empire, created by linguist Marc Okrand for Star Trek III. Agglutinative, OVS word order, no adjectives. The first constructed language designed for a major motion picture.

syntax.adjective_equivalent: Stative verbs follow the noun they modify
syntax.comparison: A Q law' B Q puS
syntax.questions: Suffix -'a' on verb, or question words (nuq, 'Iv, ghorgh, nuqDaq)
syntax.relative_clauses: -bogh on verb, head noun marked with -'e'

writing_system.name: pIqaD
writing_system.case_sensitivity: Romanization is case-sensitive: D/S are retroflex, H is velar fricative, I is near-close front, Q is uvular affricate

pragmatics.greetings: nuqneH (What do you want?) — the only greeting
pragmatics.politeness: Rudeness is default. Politeness marked by rare -neS suffix.

nodeType.name: Language
nodeType.description: A linguistic system with phonology, morphology, syntax, and writing system. Properties follow a defined structure.

target.description: The warrior language of the Klingon Empire, created by linguist Marc Okrand for Star Trek III. Agglutinative, OVS word order, no adjectives. The first constructed language designed for a major motion picture.

syntax.adjective_equivalent: Stative verbs follow the noun they modify
syntax.comparison: A Q law' B Q puS
syntax.questions: Suffix -'a' on verb, or question words (nuq, 'Iv, ghorgh, nuqDaq)
syntax.relative_clauses: -bogh on verb, head noun marked with -'e'

writing_system.name: pIqaD
writing_system.case_sensitivity: Romanization is case-sensitive: D/S are retroflex, H is velar fricative, I is near-close front, Q is uvular affricate

pragmatics.politeness: Rudeness is default. Politeness marked by rare -neS suffix.

constraints.name: Genesis Planet is unstable
constraints.description: The Genesis Planet ages and disintegrates rapidly due to David's use of protomatter. All events on the planet surface are under increasing time pressure.

scope.name: Genesis Planet

nodeType.name: Location
nodeType.description: A place where events occur.

scope.description: A newly formed planet created by the Genesis Device. Unstable due to David's use of protomatter, the planet ages rapidly and ultimately destroys itself.

hasTag.name: Sci-Fi
hasTag.description: Science fiction element or technology.

constraints.name: Katra must be reunited
constraints.description: Spock's katra in McCoy's mind must be brought to Vulcan and reunited with Spock's body via fal-tor-pan, or both McCoy and Spock's essence will be lost.

toward.name: Genesis Device

nodeType.name: Object
nodeType.description: A significant item in the world.

toward.description: A terraforming technology that reorganizes matter at the subatomic level. Created by David Marcus and Carol Marcus. Its flawed protomatter matrix causes the Genesis Planet's instability.

participants.description: Klingon commander obsessed with obtaining the Genesis secret as a weapon. Ruthless, cunning, and speaks exclusively in Klingon with his crew. Commands a Bird of Prey.

hasTag.name: Antagonist
hasTag.description: A character opposing the protagonist.

hasTag.description: A member of the Klingon species or related to Klingon culture.

location.name: Genesis Planet

nodeType.name: Location
nodeType.description: A place where events occur.

location.description: A newly formed planet created by the Genesis Device. Unstable due to David's use of protomatter, the planet ages rapidly and ultimately destroys itself.

hasTag.name: Sci-Fi
hasTag.description: Science fiction element or technology.

function.name: Climax
function.description: The peak moment of tension or conflict.

transitionType.name: Cut
transitionType.description: An abrupt transition between scenes.

threads.name: The Needs of the Few
threads.description: Inverts Spock's 'the needs of the many outweigh the needs of the few' from Wrath of Khan. Kirk argues the needs of the one — Spock — justify any sacrifice.

beatId.name: Remembering Spock
beatId.description: The film opens with a replay of Spock's death from Wrath of Khan. The Enterprise limps home. McCoy begins acting strangely.

parent.name: The Search for Spock
parent.description: Kirk risks everything to recover Spock's body from Genesis and reunite it with his katra, carried unknowingly by McCoy.

passages.content:
INT. KLINGON BIRD OF PREY - BRIDGE

KRUGE stands at the command chair. The Genesis Planet fills the viewscreen.

MALTZ
(in Klingon, subtitled)
joH, Daq Samlu'.

KRUGE
(in Klingon, subtitled)
QaQ. Genesis vIghaj vIneH. Qapla'!

ref.name: tlhIngan Hol

nodeType.name: Language
nodeType.description: A linguistic system with phonology, morphology, syntax, and writing system. Properties follow a defined structure.

ref.description: The warrior language of the Klingon Empire, created by linguist Marc Okrand for Star Trek III. Agglutinative, OVS word order, no adjectives. The first constructed language designed for a major motion picture.

syntax.adjective_equivalent: Stative verbs follow the noun they modify
syntax.comparison: A Q law' B Q puS
syntax.questions: Suffix -'a' on verb, or question words (nuq, 'Iv, ghorgh, nuqDaq)
syntax.relative_clauses: -bogh on verb, head noun marked with -'e'

writing_system.name: pIqaD
writing_system.case_sensitivity: Romanization is case-sensitive: D/S are retroflex, H is velar fricative, I is near-close front, Q is uvular affricate

pragmatics.greetings: nuqneH (What do you want?) — the only greeting
pragmatics.politeness: Rudeness is default. Politeness marked by rare -neS suffix.
